title: Oracle For Identifying Lecture Two And Three Prerequisites
summary: Detailed oracle for the dependency question that asks what cited-paper recovery work still remains beyond the promoted Lecture Two and Lecture Three flagship proofs.
assumptions: This question should surface the remaining recovery routes even though the local Witten-based theorem chains already exist.
scope: Detailed answer key for the cross-lecture prerequisite question.

source_anchors.notes: Later edge-mode arguments still depend on cited-paper recovery beyond the promoted Witten-based theorem chains.

derivation_spine: Separate the locally promoted Lecture Two theorem chain from the narrower cited TKNN and Haldane enrichment route.
Separate the locally promoted Lecture Three edge-mode chain from the cited Jackiw-Rebbi and Callan-Harvey background route.
Name the concrete open gaps and the follow-up source tasks that own those routes.
State which proof states or theorem families the recovered material must reintegrate into.
common_failure_patterns: Collapsing promoted local theorem chains and cited enrichment routes into one undifferentiated 'still missing background' claim.
Naming the open gaps without identifying the concrete follow-up source tasks or reintegration targets.
grading_rubric: Pass requires both Lecture Two and Lecture Three recovery routes to be named explicitly.
Unsafe-pass is appropriate if the answer identifies both branches but is vague about which source-fusion records or proof states they affect.
pass_conditions: The answer identifies the Lecture Two TKNN/Haldane branch and the Lecture Three Jackiw-Rebbi/Callan-Harvey branch as remaining recovery routes.
The answer routes each unresolved branch to the correct follow-up source task.
failure_triggers: Fail if the answer claims the later edge-mode story is fully source-fused without any remaining recovery work.
Fail if the answer mentions only one missing branch and hides the other.
